item.title: Apple seeks to buy memory chips from blacklisted Chinese company
item.description: The iPhone maker wants Trump administration to sign off on purchases to ease pressure from rising chip prices

item.title: Trump administration allows some access to Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: Move eases tension with AI lab but unease over Washington’s ad hoc regulatory approach remains

item.title: SpaceX bonds sell off days after AI and rocket group’s $25bn debt deal
item.description: Yields move towards levels commonly associated with junk-rated companies

item.title: S&P 500 notches longest losing streak in 10 months as chipmakers slide
item.description: Software stock-led recovery proves fleeting as Wall Street’s main indices fall for fifth consecutive session

item.title: EU border checks hit by late kit and sweaty scanners
item.description: Airports warn system could cause travel chaos during summer holiday season

item.title: Hong Kong billionaire explores sale of Harvey Nichols
item.description: Struggling luxury department store owned by Sir Dickson Poon is in active talks with multiple parties over a deal

item.title: Trump warns of 100% tariff on countries implementing digital services tax
item.description: US president says levy would supersede any trade deals Washington has with other capitals

item.title: Stockpickers: Babcock International, Telecom Plus, Moonpig
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Directors’ Deals: Dr Martens boss backs new strategy as a better fit
item.description: Group broadens footwear offering to curb its reliance on boots

item.title: Ill-timed deregulation is weakening bank resilience
item.description: Thoughtful, fundamental reform is needed in finance, not an industry checklist

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.6 to select users vetted by US government
item.description: San Francisco-based company announces ‘limited preview’ of new models with powerful cyber security capabilities

item.title: What Kevin Warsh can learn from Alan Greenspan
item.description: The new Fed chair must learn from his late predecessor’s successes — and avoid his mistakes

item.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing’s tallest building
item.description: Debris seen falling from Citic Tower after propeller aircraft hits skyscraper in Chinese capital’s central business district

item.title: Walmart’s ad deal smartly puts its customers in the shopping basket
item.description: Retailer looks to monetise its deep well of consumer data and insights

item.title: Volkswagen’s mass lay-offs are a call to action for a sleepy EU
item.description: Carmaker’s move will ratchet up the pressure on European policymakers to slap restrictions and trade barriers on China

item.title: Submit your questions: Is University Worth It?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s Working It editor at 1pm BST on Thursday July 2

item.title: UK tightens investment trust rules to close gaps exposed by Saba Capital
item.description: Watchdog’s proposals may make it harder for activist investors to control listed closed-end companies

item.title: EU plans 15% tax on aluminium scrap exports
item.description: Move by Brussels aims to protect domestic producers and prevent valuable metal heading to US and Asia

item.title: Germany urges EU to suspend methane rules after US pressure
item.description: Energy minister warns of risk to gas supplies if planned rules affecting imports come into force

item.title: Parisians seek refuge from heatwave in air-conditioned hotels
item.description: People booking rooms during the day to work while others seek night-time respite from stifling apartments

item.title: Unilever explores bid for $4bn supplement maker Thorne
item.description: Dove soap maker seeks to boost health and wellness portfolio after spinning off food business

item.title: Your summer holiday is a retirement killer
item.description: The capitalised value of that all-inclusive fortnight will scare you

item.title: Inheritance tax changes on UK pensions fuel surge in demand for annuities
item.description: Increased rates add to attractiveness of regular income

item.title: Brussels launches antitrust probe into French drugmaker Sanofi
item.description: Competition watchdog examining whether group implemented ‘misleading’ campaign against rival flu vaccine maker

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Rwanda gold refinery
item.description: Operation accused over smuggling from mineral-rich DR Congo

item.title: UK government to subsidise bank loans for green home improvements
item.description: Scheme aims to boost take-up by backing up to 20 per cent of loans for installation

item.title: German financial regulator probes Zalando over €1.1bn deal disclosure
item.description: BaFin says fashion retailer may have failed to provide information on a transaction linked to About You acquisition

item.title: Britain backs electricity storage projects as heatwave strains grid
item.description: Provisional approval of battery and hydropower projects comes after high temperatures reduce gas and solar generation

item.title: Volkswagen to axe up to 100,000 jobs in sweeping cost-cutting drive
item.description: Restructuring would remove close to one in six workers and rank among biggest corporate lay-offs of all time

item.title: Heathrow warns on global travel demand in wake of Iran conflict
item.description: UK airport lowers passenger outlook as Middle East volatility weighs on traffic volumes
